channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: How K-Pop and AI Are Reshaping the Dokdo/Takeshima Dispute
item.description: AI-generated K-pop songs about the disputed islands are turning historical grievances between South Korea and Japan into viral digital nationalism.

item.title: Japan’s Frigate Diplomacy — and What It Could Mean for Taiwan
item.description: Can Japan become a second major democratic partner for Taiwan’s defense industry?

item.title: Checks and Balance newsletter: In defence of America’s elites
item.description: John Prideaux, our US editor, on primary season

item.title: The US-China Summit from a Japanese Perspective
Japan and South Korea: Friends, With Limits
item.description: The two neighbors are drawing closer, but North Korea remains a dividing line.

item.title: Pyongyang Recalibrates: North Korea Between Russia, the United States, and Japan
item.description: Looking beyond the Russia honeymoon and Japan’s abduction diplomacy.

item.title: Japan Doubles Down on Maritime Security Cooperation
item.description: A recent visit highlights Tokyo’s seriousness.

item.title: ‘Entry Into the Kyrgyz Republic Is Closed’: US Professor Denied Entry to Kyrgyzstan
item.description: Steve Swerdlow, a well-known Central Asia researcher and a USC professor, was leading a group of 16 students to Kyrgyzstan when he was denied entry and deported.
